context: Tests whether the agent builds a Phoenix LiveView with PubSub correctly: guarding subscriptions behind connected?(socket), initializing all assigns in mount, placing broadcast logic in the context module (not the LiveView), using @impl true on all callbacks, with for multi-step operations, and returning the correct tuples from each callback type.

checklist.name: connected? guard
checklist.description: The Phoenix.PubSub.subscribe call in the LiveView's mount/3 is wrapped in an `if connected?(socket)` or equivalent guard — not called unconditionally

checklist.name: All assigns in mount
checklist.description: All assigns accessed in the template (e.g. tasks, any form/changeset assign) are initialized in mount/3, not first set in a handle_event or handle_info callback

checklist.name: No Repo in LiveView
checklist.description: The LiveView module (index.ex) does NOT contain any `Repo.` calls — all data access goes through context functions (e.g. Tasks.list_tasks/0)

checklist.name: Broadcast in context
checklist.description: The Phoenix.PubSub.broadcast call(s) are in the Tasks context module (tasks.ex), not in the LiveView module

checklist.name: @impl true on callbacks
checklist.description: Every LiveView callback (mount/3, handle_event/3, handle_info/2, and render/1 if present) is preceded by `@impl true`

checklist.name: mount returns {:ok, socket}
checklist.description: The mount/3 function returns `{:ok, socket}` (not {:noreply, socket} or a bare socket)

checklist.name: handle_event returns {:noreply, socket}
checklist.description: All handle_event/3 callbacks return `{:noreply, socket}` (not {:ok, socket})

checklist.name: with for multi-step ops
checklist.description: When create_task/1 or another fallible context function chains 2 or more sequential operations (e.g. validate then insert then broadcast), it uses a `with` expression rather than nested `case` statements

checklist.name: handle_info updates assigns
checklist.description: The LiveView has a handle_info/2 clause that matches on the PubSub broadcast message and updates the tasks assign (e.g. via assign or update) without crashing on unexpected message shapes
